title: Verify auth middleware implementation for route protection
description: The auth middleware and route protection is already implemented. Verification needed: 1) Auth middleware exists in src/middleware/auth-middleware.ts with JWT verification from Bearer token, 2) Unit tests exist in src/middleware/auth-middleware.test.ts covering valid token, missing token (401), invalid token (currently 401 but AC says 403), expired token (currently 401 but AC says 403), 3) Routes already protected with withAuth HOC: /api/courses/search (optional), /api/enroll, /api/notes, /api/notifications, /api/gradebook, /api/dashboard/admin-stats, /api/quizzes/[id]. Open question: 401 vs 403 status code alignment per discussion.
existing_patterns: src/auth/withAuth.ts - HOC pattern wrapping route handlers with JWT validation and RBAC via checkRole
src/middleware/auth-middleware.ts - createAuthMiddleware factory returning Express-style middleware function with rate limiting
src/auth/jwt-service.ts - JwtService class with sign/verify methods and TokenPayload type
src/auth/_auth.ts - extractBearerToken and checkRole utility functions
src/utils/result.ts - Result discriminated union (Ok/Err) for explicit error handling
questions: The acceptance criteria says '403 for invalid/expired token' but the existing middleware returns 401. Should expired/invalid tokens return 401 or 403? (Open question awaiting user answer per discussion)
Should the /api/health route remain unprotected for load balancer health checks?
Should /api/csrf-token remain unprotected since CSRF tokens are meant to be fetched by unauthenticated users?
